name: Chicken Enchiladas with Pasilla Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 package dried pasilla chilies, stemmed, seeded, torn into 1-inch pieces
6 cups Low-salt chicken stock or canned broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large pot over high heat. Add chilies and almonds. Saute until chilies darken and almonds are golden, about 2 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer chilies and almonds to a bowl.
2. Reduce heat to medium. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Add to the same pot and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Add stock; simmer until chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes. Transfer chicken to another bowl using a slotted spoon; cool. Reserve stock in the pot.
3. Toast cumin seeds in a heavy small skillet over medium-low heat until aromatic, about 1 minute. Mince cumin seeds. Add cumin, chili mixture, tomatoes, quartered onion, garlic, sugar, and salt to stock. Simmer until all ingredients are very soft, about 45 minutes.
4. Working in batches, puree the stock mixture in a blender. Return to pot. Boil until reduced to 4 cups, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. (Can be made 1 day ahead. Chill chicken and sauce separately.)
5. Remove skin from chicken and discard. Cut meat from bones and shred. Transfer to bowl and combine with 1/2 cup sauce. Set filling aside.
6. Oil two 13X9-inch glass baking dishes. Pour oil into a deep skillet to a depth of 1/2 inch and heat to 375°F. Fry tortillas 1 at a time until softened, about 5 seconds per side. Using a metal spatula, transfer to paper towels. Spread 1 tablespoon sauce over each tortilla. Sprinkle each with 2 tablespoons cheese and 1 tablespoon chopped onion. Place 1/3 cup chicken down the center of each tortilla; roll up. Place seam side down in baking dishes. (Can be made 1 hour ahead. Cover.)
7. Preheat oven to 350°F. Pour remaining sauce over enchiladas. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake until heated through, about 20 minutes. Top with crème fraîche, avocado slices, and cilantro sprigs.
